templates.title: Positive Student Message
templates.subject: A wonderful update about [Student Name]
templates.body:
Hello [Family Name],

I wanted to share a positive update about [Student Name]. Today, [he/she/they] showed [specific strength or behavior]. I was especially proud of [specific example].

Thank you for supporting [Student Name]. It is a joy to have [him/her/them] in class.

Warmly,
Mrs. Parrish

templates.title: Academic Progress Update
templates.subject: Academic update for [Student Name]
templates.body:
Hello [Family Name],

I wanted to provide an update on [Student Name]'s progress in [subject/skill]. [Student Name] is currently showing strength in [strength] and is continuing to work on [growth area].

At school, we are supporting this through [support or intervention]. At home, it may help to [simple home support].

Thank you for partnering with us.

Warmly,
Mrs. Parrish

templates.title: Behavior Follow-Up
templates.subject: Follow-up regarding today
templates.body:
Hello [Family Name],

I wanted to follow up about a situation involving [Student Name] today. The concern was [brief factual description]. We addressed it by [school response].

[Student Name] was given the opportunity to reflect, reset, and return to learning. Tomorrow, we will continue reinforcing [expected behavior or routine].

Please contact me with any questions.

Warmly,
Mrs. Parrish

templates.title: Attendance Reminder
templates.subject: Attendance reminder for [Student Name]
templates.body:
Hello [Family Name],

This is a friendly reminder that regular attendance is very important for [Student Name]'s academic progress. When students miss school, they also miss direct instruction, practice, and classroom routines.

Please let us know if there is anything we can do to support consistent attendance.

Thank you,
Mrs. Parrish

templates.title: Reading Intervention Update
templates.subject: Reading support update for [Student Name]
templates.body:
Hello [Family Name],

I wanted to share an update about the reading support [Student Name] is receiving. We are currently focusing on [decoding/fluency/comprehension skill] through [UFLI, MOWR, small group, rereading, etc.].

Recent progress shows [progress note]. Our next instructional step is [next step].

At home, continuing to read for 20–30 minutes and practicing [specific skill] will be helpful.

Warmly,
Mrs. Parrish

templates.title: Weekly Classroom Newsletter
templates.subject: Mrs. Parrish's Weekly Classroom Update
templates.body:
Hello Families,

Here is what we are learning this week:

Reading:
[Reading focus]

Phonics:
[Phonics focus]

Vocabulary:
[Vocabulary focus]

Math:
[Math focus]

Writing:
[Writing focus]

Science / Social Studies:
[Content focus]

Important reminders:
- [Reminder]
- [Reminder]

Thank you for your continued support!

Warmly,
Mrs. Parrish

templates.title: iDay Reminder
templates.subject: iDay reminder
templates.body:
Hello Families,

This is a reminder that [date] is an iDay. Students should complete their assigned MobyMax work and read for 20–30 minutes using Beanstack.

Please contact me if you have questions or need support accessing the assignments.

Thank you,
Mrs. Parrish

quickMessages: Your child had a wonderful day today!
Please remember to return the folder tomorrow.
We are practicing reading fluency this week.
Thank you for supporting homework and reading at home.
Please contact me when you have a moment.
Your child showed kindness and leadership today.
